items.title: Update: The Boston Celtics and Jordan Walsh have agreed to a 3-year, $16 million extension, which includes two years fully guaranteed and a third-year team option for the 2029-30 season, sources said. Boston had picked up Walsh’s $2.4 million team option for the upcoming season.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: Update: The Boston Celtics and Jordan Walsh have agreed to a 3-year, $16 million extension, which includes two years fully guaranteed and a third-year team option for the 2029-30 season, sources said. Boston had picked up Walsh’s $2.4 million team option for the upcoming season.

items.title: A well-deserved 3-year, $15M extension for Jordan Walsh.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
A well-deserved 3-year, $15M extension for Jordan Walsh.

Last season, he showcased his versatility and effectiveness while guarding players ranging from Maxey to KAT.

Now, he wants to pair his growth as a shooter & impact on the offensive glass with a new identity on that end.

Story ⬇️

items.title: [Charania] Boston Celtics forward Jordan Walsh has agreed to a three-year, $15 million contract extension with the franchise through the 2029-30 season, sources tell ESPN. Boston picked up Walsh's $2.4M option on next season and adds three more years for their 2023 second-rounder.

items.title: Jordan Walsh's contact with the Boston Celtics becomes fully guaranteed for $2.4M today.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Jordan Walsh's contact with the Boston Celtics becomes fully guaranteed for $2.4M today.

Walsh will be extension-eligible throughout the balance of the season. He can extend for up to 140% of the average player salary, which would start at $21.2M per season.

items.title: Last year, Jordan Walsh told me role player IQ and improving his jump shot were his biggest areas of growth entering a make-or-break season.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Last year, Jordan Walsh told me role player IQ and improving his jump shot were his biggest areas of growth entering a make-or-break season.

Today, I asked him about his focus this offseason, as he works to build on last year's evolution.

items.title: Jordan Walsh joins the Celtics’ Summer League contingency.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Jordan Walsh joins the Celtics’ Summer League contingency.

A chance to relish the growth from when he was playing here at this time a year ago.

items.title: Chris Cenac Jr. and Jordan Walsh are both alums of Link Academy (MO).
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Chris Cenac Jr. and Jordan Walsh are both alums of Link Academy (MO).

I asked Cenac if he knew Walsh before joining the Celtics.

“I didn't, but I'm looking forward to talking to him about his experience there for sure.”

items.title: Celtics officially announce they have exercised the team options in the contracts of Neemias Queta, Jordan Walsh, and Dalano Banton
items.author: John Karalis
items.body_excerpt: Celtics officially announce they have exercised the team options in the contracts of Neemias Queta, Jordan Walsh, and Dalano Banton

items.title: Boston picking up their team options for Neemias Queta and Jordan Walsh makes sense from managing the team salary under the tax and first apron for 2026-27.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Boston picking up their team options for Neemias Queta and Jordan Walsh makes sense from managing the team salary under the tax and first apron for 2026-27.

Both Queta and Walsh stay on a lower salary for this season and can be extended in-season to start new contracts in 2027-28.
